hero.title: 10-Year Cardiovascular Risk
hero.subtitle: Based on the General CVD risk equation

sbp.title: Blood Pressure Control
sbp.therapy1_desc: Medication review or intensification can lower systolic blood pressure; randomized evidence shows fewer cardiovascular events per 5 mmHg SBP reduction.
sbp.therapy2_desc: DASH nutrition, sodium reduction, weight management, regular activity, and lower alcohol intake can move blood pressure toward the healthy range.

totalChol.title: Total Cholesterol Reduction
totalChol.therapy1_desc: Statins are first-line for cardiovascular risk reduction; each 1 mmol/L LDL-C reduction lowers major vascular events by about 22%.
totalChol.therapy2_desc: Replacing saturated fat, increasing fiber, and improving overall dietary quality supports lower atherogenic cholesterol.

cholHDL.title: HDL Support
cholHDL.therapy1_desc: Aerobic and combined training can modestly raise HDL and improves cardiovascular risk through several pathways.
cholHDL.therapy2_desc: Weight management, smoking cessation, and triglyceride/LDL management are preferred; medication solely to raise HDL has not reliably reduced events.

smoking.title: Smoking Cessation
smoking.therapy1_desc: Behavioral counseling plus nicotine replacement, varenicline, or bupropion improves quit success and reduces cardiovascular risk.
smoking.therapy2_desc: Avoiding relapse is important because cardiovascular risk falls within years after quitting but can remain elevated for a decade or more.

timeline.title: Risk Timeline
timeline.hint: Simulate treatments to track risk changes over time
timeline.empty: Set a baseline and add snapshots to track your risk over scenarios.

profileWarning.title: Reset All Values?
profileWarning.message: You have unsaved changes. Resetting will discard your current data.
profileWarning.message2: Resetting will discard your current data.

simulationCloseWarning.message: You saved a profile. The simulation is still active. What would you like to do?
simulationCloseWarning.messageBoth: You saved both profiles. Which would you like to load?
